title: OAuth Token Leakage via Redirect URI Bypass
description: Unauthorized access tokens were issued to an attacker-controlled endpoint due to insufficient redirect URI validation in the OAuth authorization server. Approximately 12,000 customer records were potentially exposed. This was identified during the Q1 2024 security audit (SA-2024-Q1).
root_cause: Regex-based redirect URI validation allowed partial wildcard matches, accepting attacker-controlled subdomains like techcorp.com.attacker.com as valid redirect targets.
resolution: Deployed strict exact-match redirect URI validation. Revoked all tokens issued between January 12 and February 3. Enforced PKCE for all public clients. Ref: CVE-2024-1001.

title: Misconfigured S3 Bucket Exposing Analytics Data
description: Two S3 buckets (techcorp-analytics-raw, techcorp-ml-training-data) were publicly accessible due to overly permissive bucket policies introduced during a cross-account access configuration change. Behavioral analytics data for 450,000+ users was potentially exposed.
root_cause: Data Engineering team modified bucket policies to enable cross-account access for an analytics partner, inadvertently granting s3:GetObject to the wildcard (*) principal.
resolution: Corrected bucket policies to named IAM roles only. Enabled S3 Block Public Access at the account level. Deployed AWS Config rule with automated remediation. GDPR Article 33 notification submitted to ICO. Ref: CVE-2024-1003.

title: Unauthenticated API Endpoints Discovered on Kong Gateway
description: Five API endpoints on the Kong API Gateway were exposed without authentication due to a merge conflict in the Kong declarative configuration that removed the jwt-auth plugin from several routes. An estimated 3,200 unauthorized API calls were detected from 17 unique IPs.
root_cause: Jenkins CI/CD pipeline deployed a kong.yml configuration containing a merge conflict from February 14, 2024 that silently dropped the jwt-auth plugin from five routes.
resolution: Re-enabled authentication plugins on all affected routes. Implemented default-deny authentication policy at gateway level. Added CI/CD pipeline validation to verify auth plugin presence before deployment. Ref: CVE-2024-1002.

title: Brute Force Attack on OAuth Token Endpoint
description: Automated credential stuffing attack detected against the /oauth/token endpoint originating from a botnet of 2,300 IPs. Over 1.2 million authentication attempts were made over a 48-hour period. No rate limiting was in place to mitigate the attack.
root_cause: Missing rate limiting and anomaly detection on authentication endpoints. The absence of these controls was flagged as CVE-2024-1006 in the Q1 2024 audit but had not yet been remediated.
resolution: Emergency rate limiting deployed (10 req/min/IP). CAPTCHA enabled after 3 consecutive failures. WAF rules updated to block known botnet IP ranges. 47 compromised accounts identified and credentials reset.

title: Access Token Theft via XSS in Customer Portal
description: A stored XSS vulnerability in the customer support portal allowed an attacker to inject JavaScript that exfiltrated OAuth access tokens stored in localStorage. Eight customer accounts were compromised before detection.
root_cause: The customer portal SPA stored OAuth access tokens in localStorage instead of using the BFF pattern or memory-only storage. A stored XSS vulnerability in the support ticket description field (inadequate input sanitization) enabled token exfiltration.
resolution: Patched the XSS vulnerability with proper input sanitization and CSP headers. Migrated token storage from localStorage to the BFF pattern per OAuth Security Guidelines Section 5.1. Revoked all affected tokens and notified impacted customers.

title: Legacy CRM MD5 Password Hash Database Dump
description: An SQL injection vulnerability in the legacy CRM system allowed extraction of the user credentials table containing 8,500 passwords hashed with unsalted MD5. Given the weak hashing, approximately 67% of passwords were recoverable within hours using rainbow tables.
root_cause: Legacy CRM (legacy-crm.techcorp.internal) used parameterized queries inconsistently. The user search endpoint was vulnerable to SQL injection. Passwords were stored with unsalted MD5 as identified in CVE-2024-1004.
resolution: Patched SQL injection vulnerability. Force-reset all CRM user passwords. Accelerated migration to Okta SSO with MFA. Decommissioned legacy credential store. GDPR Article 33 notification submitted for affected EU users.

title: TLS 1.0 Downgrade Attack on Legacy HR System
description: Monitoring detected a TLS downgrade attempt against the legacy HR system exploiting the continued support for TLS 1.0. While the attack was not successful in intercepting data, it confirmed the risk identified in CVE-2024-1007.
root_cause: Legacy HR system maintained TLS 1.0/1.1 support for backward compatibility with a third-party payroll integration that does not support TLS 1.2+.
resolution: Deployed a TLS-terminating reverse proxy in front of the legacy HR system that enforces TLS 1.2+ for all connections except the specific payroll vendor IP range. Vendor contacted for upgrade timeline (expected Q4 2024).

title: Kubernetes Privilege Escalation via Default Service Account
description: During penetration testing for the Q3 2024 audit, auditors demonstrated privilege escalation from a compromised low-privilege pod to cluster-admin access in under 4 minutes using the default service account token in the production namespace.
root_cause: The default service account in the production namespace was bound to a ClusterRole with pods/exec and secrets access. Automatic service account token mounting was not disabled, allowing any compromised pod to leverage these permissions. Ref: CVE-2024-2001.
resolution: Removed ClusterRoleBindings from default service accounts. Set automountServiceAccountToken: false on all workloads not requiring API access. Deployed OPA Gatekeeper policies to prevent overly permissive RBAC bindings.

title: Container Image with Critical CVE Deployed to Production
description: The payment-service deployment was found running a container image based on node:16-alpine containing CVE-2023-44487 (HTTP/2 Rapid Reset) and CVE-2024-21896 (path traversal). No image scanning was in place to block vulnerable images.
root_cause: Absence of container image vulnerability scanning in the CI/CD pipeline. Base images were not regularly updated. No admission controller to validate image security before deployment. Ref: CVE-2024-2002.
resolution: Updated payment-service base image to node:20-alpine. Integrated Trivy scanning into GitLab CI pipeline with blocking policy for Critical/High CVEs. Deployed Kyverno admission controller for image signature verification.

title: Unauthorized Access to Elasticsearch Cluster from K8s Pods
description: The Elasticsearch cluster used for centralized logging was accessible without authentication from any pod within the Kubernetes network due to missing network policies and no Elasticsearch security configuration. A developer's debug pod was found to have queried sensitive audit logs.
root_cause: Elasticsearch deployed without X-Pack Security authentication. No Kubernetes NetworkPolicy restricted access to the logging namespace. Any pod in the cluster could query the Elasticsearch API directly. Ref: CVE-2024-2008.
resolution: Enabled X-Pack Security on Elasticsearch with role-based access. Deployed NetworkPolicies restricting access to the logging namespace to only Fluentd, Kibana, and the Security Operations service account. Revoked the debug pod's access.

title: Stale IAM Keys Used from Unauthorized Location
description: AWS GuardDuty detected API calls using IAM access keys belonging to a former employee (departed 7 months prior) originating from an IP address in a country where TechCorp has no operations. The keys had not been rotated or deactivated upon departure.
root_cause: Employee offboarding process did not include IAM access key deactivation. The Q3 2024 audit identified 12 stale IAM keys older than 180 days (CVE-2024-2009), including 4 associated with departed employees.
resolution: Immediately deactivated all 12 stale IAM keys. Implemented automated IAM key rotation policy (90-day maximum age). Added IAM key deactivation to the employee offboarding checklist. CloudTrail forensic analysis confirmed no data exfiltration beyond S3 listing operations.

title: Secrets Exposed in Kubernetes Deployment Manifests
description: Eight Kubernetes deployments were found storing database passwords, API keys, and encryption keys as plain-text environment variables in Deployment manifests. These values were visible via kubectl describe pod and stored unencrypted in etcd.
root_cause: Development teams used environment variables for convenience without following the Kubernetes Security Guide requirements for secrets management. No policy enforcement prevented this anti-pattern. Ref: CVE-2024-2005.
resolution: Migrated all affected deployments to use Kubernetes Secrets with etcd encryption enabled. Initiated HashiCorp Vault deployment for centralized secrets management. Deployed OPA Gatekeeper policy to block deployments with secrets in environment variables.

title: GDPR Data Subject Access Request Missed SLA
description: A data subject access request (DSAR) from an EU customer was not processed within the 30-day GDPR Article 15 deadline due to the request being routed to the wrong internal team. The delay was 47 days from receipt to fulfillment.
root_cause: The DSAR intake form routed requests to the Customer Support team instead of the DPO office. The Customer Support team was not trained on GDPR timelines and did not escalate the request.
resolution: Updated DSAR routing to direct all requests to dpo@techcorp.internal. Implemented automated SLA tracking with alerts at 15-day and 25-day thresholds. Conducted GDPR training for Customer Support team. Apologized to the data subject and provided the requested data.

title: Jenkins Default Credentials on Development Instance
description: The development Jenkins instance was found running with default administrator credentials (admin/admin). While only accessible from the VPN, this violated TechCorp's security policy and was flagged during the Q1 2024 audit.
root_cause: Development Jenkins instance was provisioned using an outdated Terraform module that did not include credential randomization. The default credentials were never changed post-deployment. Ref: CVE-2024-1011.
resolution: Changed Jenkins admin credentials. Updated the Terraform module to generate random credentials stored in Vault. Added automated compliance check for default credentials across all development tooling.

title: Log Injection Attack Masking Unauthorized Billing API Access
description: An attacker exploited log injection vulnerabilities to forge legitimate-looking log entries and mask unauthorized API calls to the billing service. The attack went undetected for 12 days because injected log fields disguised malicious traffic as health checks, bypassing SIEM detection rules.
root_cause: Application services write user-controlled input directly into structured log fields without sanitization, allowing injection of newline characters and valid JSON structures. The attacker injected severity:DEBUG and source:healthcheck fields to evade alerting thresholds. Ref: CVE-2024-2003.
resolution: Investigation ongoing. Immediate actions: deployed input sanitization on log fields for the billing service. Implementing structured logging with parameterized fields across all services. Forensic analysis of 90-day log history in progress to determine full scope of unauthorized access.

title: OAuth Refresh Token Reuse Detected — Potential Token Theft
description: The refresh token rotation mechanism detected reuse of a previously invalidated refresh token for 3 user accounts within a 2-hour window. This triggered automatic token family revocation and a security alert indicating potential token theft.
root_cause: Malware on three users' devices was exfiltrating refresh tokens from the mobile app's secure storage. The tokens were replayed from a different IP address after the legitimate device had already rotated them.
resolution: Revoked all token families for affected users. Forced password reset and re-authentication. Notified affected users of potential device compromise. Enhanced geographic anomaly detection to flag refresh token usage from different countries within 1 hour.

title: Kubernetes Pod Running as Root in Production
description: The Q3 2024 audit identified 15 pods in the production cluster running as root with privileged:true security context, violating the Kubernetes Security Guide's pod security standards. No Pod Security Admission enforcement was in place.
root_cause: Pod Security Admission controller was not configured in enforce mode for production namespaces. Development teams deployed containers with default (root) user without being blocked. Ref: CVE-2024-2006.
resolution: Enabled Pod Security Admission in enforce mode with the restricted profile for all production namespaces. Updated affected container images to run as non-root. Deployed Kyverno policies as additional enforcement layer.

title: Internal mTLS Not Enforced — HTTP Traffic Between Services
description: Approximately 40% of internal microservice communication in the production Kubernetes cluster was occurring over plain HTTP. The Istio service mesh was deployed but mTLS enforcement was set to PERMISSIVE mode instead of STRICT, as identified in the Q1 2024 audit.
root_cause: Istio mTLS was deployed in PERMISSIVE mode to avoid breaking services during initial mesh rollout. The migration to STRICT mode was deprioritized due to concerns about backward compatibility with services not yet onboarded to the mesh. Ref: SA-2024-Q1 Section 5.3.
resolution: Migrated all services to Istio sidecar injection. Enabled STRICT mTLS enforcement mesh-wide. Added PeerAuthentication policy requiring mTLS in all production namespaces. Verified with network traffic analysis — zero plain HTTP inter-service communication confirmed.

title: Helm Charts Pulled Over Insecure HTTP in CI/CD
description: The CI/CD pipeline was found pulling Helm charts from two community repositories using HTTP instead of HTTPS, exposing the pipeline to man-in-the-middle attacks that could inject malicious chart content. Ref: CVE-2024-2007.
root_cause: Helm repository URLs in the GitLab CI configuration file used HTTP for two community chart repositories. No integrity verification (chart provenance/signatures) was configured.
resolution: Updated Helm repository URLs to HTTPS. Working on implementing chart provenance verification using Helm provenance files. Auditing all CI/CD pipeline configurations for similar insecure transport issues.

title: GDPR Article 34 Notification Required for OAuth Breach
description: Following the OAuth token leakage incident (INC-001) and the DPO's breach assessment, it was determined that the breach posed high risk to the rights and freedoms of the 12,000 affected data subjects, requiring notification under GDPR Article 34 in addition to the Article 33 supervisory authority notification.
root_cause: The OAuth token leakage (CVE-2024-1001) exposed customer PII including names, emails, and billing addresses. The DPO assessed this as high risk due to the nature of the data and the potential for identity theft.
resolution: Article 33 notification submitted to ICO on March 18, 2024. Article 34 notification emails sent to all 12,000 affected data subjects on March 22, 2024. Credit monitoring offered to affected customers. Public statement published on March 25, 2024, coordinated with Communications Lead per Incident Response Policy.
